title: Habakkuk 1
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Habakkuk
paragraphs: 1 This is the message that Habakkuk the prophet received in a vision:
2 How long, O LORD, must I call for help, but you do not hear? Or cry out to you, "Violence!" but you do not save?
3 Why do you make me see sin? Why do you allow wrong? Destruction and violence are before me. Fighting goes on, and conflict abounds.
4 So the law has no power, and justice never goes out. The wicked trap the righteous, so justice is twisted.
5 "Look at the nations and watch — be greatly amazed! For I am doing a work in your days that you would not believe, even if someone told you.
6 Look, I am raising up the Chaldeans — that cruel and quick nation. They march across the earth to take homes not their own.
7 They are feared and dreaded. From themselves they make their own justice and rule.
8 Their horses are faster than leopards, fiercer than wolves at night. Their horsemen charge ahead. Their cavalry comes from far away. They fly like a vulture, swooping down to eat.
9 All of them come for violence. Their crowds advance like the east wind. They gather prisoners like sand.
10 They mock kings and laugh at rulers. They laugh at every strong fort and build siege ramps to take it.
11 Then they sweep by like the wind and pass through. They are guilty. Their own strength is their god."
12 Are you not from forever, O LORD, my God, my Holy One? We will not die. O LORD, you have appointed them to bring judgment. O Rock, you have set them up to correct us.
13 Your eyes are too pure to look on evil. You cannot tolerate wrong. So why do you tolerate the faithless? Why are you silent while the wicked swallow up those more righteous than themselves?
14 You have made men like the fish of the sea, like crawling things that have no ruler.
15 The enemy pulls all of them up with a hook. He catches them in his dragnet and gathers them in his fishing net. So he is happy and glad.
16 So he sacrifices to his dragnet and burns incense to his fishing net. For by these his food is rich and his portion is great.
17 Will he then empty his net and keep on killing nations without mercy?

title: Habakkuk 2
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Habakkuk
paragraphs: 1 I will stand at my watch post and stay on the wall. I will watch to see what he will say to me, and how I should answer when corrected.
2 Then the LORD answered me: "Write down this vision and clearly carve it on tablets, so that a runner may run with it.
3 For the vision waits for the right time. It speaks of the end and does not lie. Though it lingers, wait for it. It will surely come and will not delay.
4 Look at the proud one. His soul is not upright. But the righteous will live by faith.

5 Wealth betrays him. He is a proud man, never at rest. He enlarges his appetite like the grave. Like Death, he is never satisfied. He gathers all the nations to himself and collects all peoples as his own.

6 Will not all these take up a mocking song against him? They speak with mockery and contempt: 'Woe to him who piles up what is not his and makes himself rich with many loans! How long will this go on?'
7 Will not those you owe suddenly rise up? Those who disturb you will wake up. Then you will become their prey.
8 Because you have robbed many nations, the survivors of the people will rob you. This is because of your bloodshed against man and your violence against the land, the city, and all who live there.
9 Woe to him who builds his house by unjust money, to put his nest on high and escape disaster!
10 You have planned shame for your house by cutting off many peoples and losing your own life.
11 For the stones will cry out from the wall, and the wooden beams will echo it from the woodwork.
12 Woe to him who builds a city with bloodshed and sets up a town by wrongdoing!
13 Is it not from the LORD of Hosts that the labor of the people only feeds the fire? The nations wear themselves out in vain.
14 For the earth will be filled with the knowledge of the LORD's glory, as the waters cover the sea.
15 Woe to him who gives drink to his neighbors, pouring it from the wineskin until they are drunk, in order to look at their nakedness!
16 You will be filled with shame instead of glory. You too must drink and show your uncircumcision! The cup in the LORD's right hand will come around to you. Utter shame will cover your glory.

17 Your violence against Lebanon will overwhelm you. The destruction of animals will terrify you. This is because of your bloodshed against men and your violence against the land, the city, and all who live there.
18 What good is an idol that a craftsman carves? Or an image, a teacher of lies? For its maker trusts in his own creation. He makes idols that cannot speak.
19 Woe to him who says to wood, 'Wake up!' Or to silent stone, 'Arise!' Can it give guidance? Look, it is covered with gold and silver, yet there is no breath in it at all."
20 But the LORD is in his holy temple. Let all the earth be silent before him.

title: Habakkuk 3
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Habakkuk
paragraphs: 1 This is a prayer of Habakkuk the prophet, according to Shigionoth:
2 O LORD, I have heard the news about you. I stand in awe, O LORD, of your deeds. Bring them to life again in these years. Make them known in these years. In your anger, remember mercy!
3 God came from Teman, and the Holy One from Mount Paran. Selah His glory covered the heavens, and his praise filled the earth.
4 His brightness was like the sunlight. Rays flashed from his hand, where his power is hidden.
5 Plague went before him, and fever followed in his steps.
6 He stood and measured the earth. He looked and shook the nations. The ancient mountains crumbled. The age-old hills sank down. His ways are everlasting.
7 I saw the tents of Cushan in distress. The curtains of Midian were trembling.
8 Were you angry at the rivers, O LORD? Was your wrath against the streams? Did you rage against the sea when you rode on your horses, on your chariots of salvation?
9 You held up your bow. You called for many arrows. Selah You split the earth with rivers.
10 The mountains saw you and shook. Floods of water swept by. The deep roared with its voice and lifted its hands on high.
11 Sun and moon stood still in their places at the flash of your flying arrows, at the brightness of your shining spear.
12 You marched across the earth in anger. You crushed the nations in wrath.
13 You went out for the salvation of your people, to save your anointed one. You crushed the head of the house of the wicked. You stripped him from head to toe. Selah
14 With his own spear you pierced his head, when his warriors stormed out to scatter us. They gloated as if ready to secretly devour the weak.
15 You trampled the sea with your horses, churning the great waters.
16 I heard and trembled within. My lips quivered at the sound. Decay entered my bones. I trembled where I stood. Yet I must wait patiently for the day of distress to come on the people who invade us.

17 Though the fig tree does not bud and no fruit is on the vines, though the olive crop fails and the fields produce no food, though the sheep are cut off from the fold and no cattle are in the stalls,
18 yet I will rejoice in the LORD. I will be glad in the God of my salvation!
19 GOD the Lord is my strength. He makes my feet like those of a deer. He makes me walk upon the heights! For the choirmaster. With stringed instruments.
